originalTitle: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
title: Big News from Kenya: Talking About Staying Safe and Healthy!
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! It's me, your friendly news reporter, here to share an important story from a beautiful country we all know: Kenya! Kenya is a place with amazing animals like lions and elephants, and bustling cities too. Today, we're going to talk about something that many people in Kenya are thinking and talking about.

A Special Plan in Kenya

Imagine your community, your village, or your town. Everyone wants to be safe and happy, right? Well, in some parts of Kenya, people are talking about a special plan. This plan is about helping people who might get very sick with something called Ebola.

What is Ebola?

Now, "Ebola" might sound like a big, scary word, but it's just a name for a type of sickness. It's like when you get a cold or a flu, but Ebola can make people much sicker. It's not a sickness that spreads easily just by talking or sitting near someone for a little bit. Doctors and nurses know how to take care of people with Ebola very carefully. **Did you know?** Washing your hands with soap and water is one of the best ways to keep germs away and stay healthy! It's like magic for your hands!

A Special Place to Get Better

So, what's the plan? Some kind people from another country, the United States, want to help Kenya build a special place. Think of it like a special hospital, but just for people who have Ebola. It's called a "treatment facility" or a "quarantine centre." The idea is that sick people can go there, get lots of help from doctors and nurses, and get better. This place would also help make sure the sickness doesn't spread to other people.

Why Are People Talking So Much?

Now, even though this sounds like a good idea to help people, some people in Kenya are very worried. They live near where this special place might be built. They are worried that if this place is too close to their homes, the sickness might spread to them or their families. They are worried for their children and their friends. It's natural to feel worried when something new and big is planned near your home, especially if it's about sickness. **Did you know?** Kenya is famous for its amazing wildlife safaris! You can see zebras, giraffes, and even rhinos roaming free in its national parks!

Showing They Are Worried

Because they are so worried, some people decided to show how they feel. They gathered together to talk and tell everyone that they are not happy about the plan. This is called a "demonstration" or a "protest." It's when many people come together to show their leaders what they are thinking and hoping for. They want their voices to be heard. They hope that by showing their worries, the leaders will listen and change the plan.

Sad News and Big Feelings

Sometimes, when people are very worried and upset, things can become very sad. In these gatherings, some people got hurt, and sadly, some people lost their lives. This made many people even sadder and angrier. It shows how strong feelings can be when people are worried about their safety and their homes. It's always important to remember that everyone wants to feel safe and protected, especially their families. **Did you know?** The capital city of Kenya is Nairobi, a big, busy city that is home to many people and even a national park with wild animals right outside the city!

Understanding Different Feelings

It's like when you and your friend both want to play with the same toy. You both have strong feelings! In this story, the people who want to build the special place want to help. They think it's the best way to keep everyone safe from Ebola. But the people who live nearby are worried about their own families and homes. Both groups have important reasons for how they feel. It's important to try and understand everyone's side of the story.

What Can We Learn?

When there are big worries and different feelings, what's the best thing to do? The best thing is to talk. To listen to each other. To try and understand why someone is worried. If we talk calmly and listen carefully, we can often find new ways to solve problems that make everyone feel safer and happier. **Did you know?** Many scientists and doctors from Africa and all over the world work very hard every day to find ways to make people healthy and strong. They are real-life superheroes!

Finding Solutions Together

Leaders in Kenya and the kind people from the United States are now talking. They are listening to the worries of the people. They want to find a solution that helps sick people get better *and* makes sure everyone else feels safe in their homes. It's like when you and your friends try to find a game everyone likes to play. You have to talk and share ideas! Finding a good solution means everyone might have to compromise a little bit.

Being Good Neighbours

This story reminds us that being a good neighbour is very important. Not just to the people next door, but to everyone in our community and even people from other countries. We all want to live in a world where everyone is healthy, safe, and happy. It might take some time and a lot of talking, but working together is the best way to get there. Our leaders are trying to do their best to make sure everyone is safe and happy. **Did you know?** Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries, each with its own special stories, cultures, and amazing people!

Staying Strong and Hopeful

So, even when there are big worries, we must remember to stay strong and hopeful. People in Kenya are strong, and they care a lot about their communities. By talking, listening, and working together, they can find a path forward that brings peace and health to everyone. Let's send our good wishes to them!
summary: People in Kenya are talking a lot about a plan to build a special place for sick people, and some are worried and want to make sure everyone stays safe.
whyMatters: It's important to understand why people feel the way they do when big plans are made, and how we can all work together by talking and listening to keep everyone healthy and happy in our communities and beyond.

originalTitle: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
title: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
content: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

originalTitle: Ghana's anti-LGBTQ+ bill to be scrutinised before approval, president says
title: Ghana's anti-LGBTQ+ bill to be scrutinised before approval, president says
content: Passed by MPs, the bill proposes prison sentences for people identifying as part of the LGBTQ+ community.

originalTitle: UN tells world to brace for extreme weather as El Nino looms
title: Big Weather Changes Are Coming! Let's Get Ready!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friend, Reporter Malaika, here with some important news all the way from Africa! Have you ever noticed how the weather changes? Sometimes it's super sunny and warm, and other times it's rainy. Well, grown-ups all over the world, including here in our wonderful Africa, are talking about some *extra big* weather changes that might happen soon. Don't worry, we're going to talk about it together, so you understand everything and know how to stay safe and help your family. It's like preparing for a big adventure, but this adventure is about our weather!

What is This Big Weather Change?

There's a special name for this big weather change. It's called 'El Niño'! Can you say that? El-Nee-Nyo. It sounds a bit like 'a little boy' in Spanish! El Niño is like when a very big part of the ocean, especially the Pacific Ocean, gets a little warmer than usual. Imagine if your bath water suddenly got super warm – that's a bit like what happens in a huge part of the ocean. This warm water in the ocean can change how the air moves all around our whole planet. And guess what? This change in the air can make our weather here in Africa, and in many other places, behave differently. Sometimes it makes the sun shine too much, making everything dry. Other times, it brings too much rain! Did you know? The Pacific Ocean is the biggest ocean in the world! It's so big, it covers almost one-third of our Earth. Imagine how much water that is!

Sometimes Too Much Water: Floods!

One of the things El Niño can bring is *floods*. What are floods? Floods happen when it rains, and rains, and rains, until there is too much water everywhere! The rivers get super full and might overflow their banks. Then, water starts to cover roads, fields, and sometimes even people's homes. Imagine your favorite playground suddenly turning into a big muddy pond! That's what floods can feel like. When floods happen, it can be hard to go to school, or for parents to go to work. It can also make our crops, the yummy food we grow on farms, get spoiled by all the water. This can be very tricky for families and communities. That's why grown-ups want us to be prepared. Did you know? Rivers and lakes in Africa are home to many amazing animals like hippos and crocodiles! Floods can sometimes make these animals move to new places, which can be surprising.

Sometimes Not Enough Water: Drought!

On the other side of things, El Niño can also bring *drought*. Drought is when there isn't enough rain for a very, very long time. Imagine if you were super thirsty, but there was no water to drink. That's what drought is like for our land and everything on it! The sun shines super brightly every day, and the sky is clear, but no rain clouds come to bring us water. The ground gets very dry and dusty, plants and trees can't grow properly, and our animals don't have enough water to drink. This makes it very hard for farmers to grow food, and sometimes people have to walk very far to find water. Water is super precious, isn't it? We need it to live, to grow food, and to keep our animals healthy. Did you know? Many parts of Africa have very dry seasons. During a drought, these dry times get even harder. A special African tree called the baobab can store water in its big trunk to survive long dry spells! It's like a living water tank!

Super Hot Days: Heatwaves!

Another thing El Niño can bring is *heatwaves*. A heatwave is when the weather gets super, super hot for many days in a row. It's like the sun decides to turn up its heater very high! Imagine trying to play football or jump rope when it's just too warm – it makes you feel tired very quickly. When it's a heatwave, it can be hard to play outside because it's just too warm. We need to drink lots and lots of water to stay cool and healthy. Animals also feel the heat! They look for shade under trees, just like we do. It's important to keep ourselves hydrated and listen to our parents when they say it's too hot to play outside for too long. Did you know? The Sahara Desert in Africa is one of the hottest places on Earth! But even in places that are not deserts, heatwaves can make life tricky and we need to be careful.

Why Grown-Ups Are Talking About This Now

The grown-ups at the United Nations, which is like a big club of countries working together (and yes, our country here in Africa is part of it!), are saying that this El Niño might be extra strong this year. This means the floods, droughts, and heatwaves could be bigger than usual. They want everyone to know so that we can all be ready. When we know what might happen, we can prepare better! Our leaders, scientists, and communities are thinking about how to help people, how to protect our farms, and how to make sure everyone has what they need if the weather gets tricky. They are like very clever planners, working to keep us all safe.

How Can We Help and Stay Safe?

Even though we are kids, we are super important and we can still help! Here are some ways: 1. **Listen to your parents and teachers:** They will tell you how to stay safe, like if it's too hot to play outside, or where to go if there's too much rain. They are the best guides! 2. **Save water:** If there's a drought, every drop of water counts! Turn off the tap when you brush your teeth. Don't leave water running. Every little bit helps! 3. **Learn more:** Ask questions! The more you know about El Niño and our weather, the better you can understand what's happening around you. Learning is powerful! 4. **Help others:** If you see someone who needs help, especially if they are very young or very old, tell a grown-up. We are a community, and we help each other. 5. **Be kind to our planet:** Remember to reuse things, reduce what you use, and recycle. These small things help our Earth stay healthy in the long run. A healthy Earth helps us all. Did you know? Saving water is super important, especially for animals too! A big elephant, for example, can drink up to 200 liters of water in just one day! So, imagine how much water all the animals need! So, my dear friends, El Niño is a big change in our weather, but we are strong and smart! By listening, learning, and helping, we can all get through these big weather changes together. Reporter Malaika signing off! Stay safe, keep smiling, and be kind to our amazing planet!
summary: Grown-ups are saying a weather change called El Niño might bring big floods, droughts, and hot weather to Africa and the world, so we need to learn and be ready to stay safe.
whyMatters: This is important because knowing about El Niño helps us prepare for big changes in rain and heat, so we can stay safe, protect our farms, and help our communities.

originalTitle: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
title: Africa's Big Shopping Adventure with China!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Guess what? I have some super exciting news for our wonderful continent! Imagine a giant, bustling marketplace where everyone loves to share and swap things. Well, something like that is happening right now, and it's really good for Africa! Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries, all full of amazing people and beautiful places!

A Special Door Opens!

Imagine Africa is like a big group of friends who make lots of wonderful things. And China is a very, very big country far away, across the ocean. China has a huge market, like a giant shop, where people buy and sell things all day long. For a long time, when things made in Africa wanted to be sold in China, there was a tiny problem. China used to ask for a little extra money, like a small entrance fee, for some of Africa's products. This extra money is called a 'tariff.' It made African things a little more expensive for people in China to buy. But guess what fantastic news? China has decided to stop asking for that extra money for many, many things that Africa makes! It's like they opened a special 'no extra fee' door just for Africa's goodies! Did you know? A 'tariff' is like a special tax or fee that countries sometimes add to things they buy from other countries. It can make those things more expensive!

What Amazing Things Does Africa Make?

Our continent, Africa, is bursting with creativity and wonderful resources! We grow delicious fruits like juicy mangoes, sweet pineapples, and yummy bananas. We also grow coffee beans to make your parents' morning drink, and cocoa beans that turn into tasty chocolate! Beyond food, talented people in Africa make beautiful handmade crafts, colourful clothes with unique patterns, and lovely wooden carvings. We also have special rocks and minerals from deep in the earth. These are all called 'products' because they are things we make or find! Now, with China saying 'no extra fee,' it means all these wonderful things from Africa can be sold more easily and cheaply in China. Imagine millions of people in China enjoying African coffee or wearing African-made clothes! How cool is that? Did you know? 'Exports' are things a country sells to other countries, and 'imports' are things a country buys from other countries. We want Africa to export more!

Why is This Good News for Africa?

When Africa can sell more of its products to a big country like China, lots of good things happen right here at home. Think about it: * **More Jobs!** If more people want African mangoes, farmers will need more helpers to pick them. If more people want African clothes, more tailors will be busy sewing! More jobs mean more people can earn money to take care of their families. * **More Money for Our Countries!** When businesses sell more, they make more money. This money can help our governments build new schools so more kids can learn. It can help build new hospitals so people can get better when they are sick. It can even help build better roads and bridges! * **Africa Grows Stronger!** When our countries make and sell more of their own things, it helps us grow stronger and more independent. We learn new ways to make things, we get better at selling them, and we show the world how amazing African businesses are. This is what we call 'industrial growth' – making more things in factories and farms! This special 'no extra fee' deal gives us all new 'hopes' that Africa can grow even faster and become even better for everyone. Did you know? When a country makes more things itself, it means it's becoming stronger and can rely less on other countries for everything!

But Wait, Let's Make It Fair!

Even though this is wonderful news, sometimes things between countries can still be a bit 'uneven.' Imagine you and your friend are swapping stickers. If you always give your friend your best, shiny stickers, but they only give you their plain ones, that wouldn't feel very fair, would it? 'Trade' between countries can sometimes be like that. Even with the 'no extra fee' pass, some people worry that China might still buy many more things from Africa than China buys from Africa. Or, China might buy mostly the 'raw' things from Africa – like just the cocoa beans or just the rocks – instead of the finished things, like chocolate bars or beautiful jewellery made here in Africa. Africa wants to grow not just by selling raw materials, but by making and selling amazing finished products too! We want to make our own chocolate, our own clothes, our own furniture right here in Africa, and then sell them to the world. That way, we get more money and create even more jobs for our people. Did you know? Selling a finished product, like a chocolate bar, often earns more money than just selling the raw ingredients, like cocoa beans! It adds 'value' to the product!

Africa's Bright Future!

So, while this 'no extra fee' pass from China is a really good step, we also want to make sure that our 'trade' becomes more and more 'even' and fair for everyone. We want Africa to keep growing, building more factories, creating more jobs, and making more wonderful things to share with the whole world. Our continent is full of smart people, beautiful land, and big, big dreams. This new news is a reason to be hopeful and proud of Africa! We are strong, we are creative, and we are ready to shine! Did you know? Africa is home to some of the fastest-growing economies in the world! That means many countries here are getting richer and stronger very quickly! Let's all cheer for Africa and its amazing future! Woohoo!
summary: China is letting many African products be sold without extra fees, which gives Africa a better chance to grow and create more jobs, but we still need to make sure trade is fair for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because it means African countries can sell more of their products to a very big country like China, helping our continent grow stronger, create more jobs for people, and build better schools and hospitals for everyone.

originalTitle: Intisar Shanib becomes first woman to head football club in Libya
title: Intisar Shanib becomes first woman to head football club in Libya
content: Shanib has been appointed as the president of Darnes Sports Club, a prominent football club in Libya's Derna city.
